commands.name: checkpoint_audit
commands.shell: python3 scripts/nvfp4_checkpoint_audit.py --model-dir ${GEMMA_NVFP4_MODEL_DIR} --output results/${RUN_ID}_nvfp4_checkpoint_audit.json --strict

commands.name: start_server
commands.shell: python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model-path ${GEMMA_NVFP4_MODEL_DIR} --attention-backend triton --dtype bfloat16 --host 0.0.0.0 --port 30000

commands.name: record_row
commands.shell: python3 scripts/record_openai_serving_row.py --backend sglang --phase exploratory --run-id ${RUN_ID} --url http://127.0.0.1:30000 --model ${GEMMA_NVFP4_MODEL_ID} --quantization nvfp4 --kv-cache-dtype bf16-or-fp8 --attention-backend triton --cuda-graph-mode enabled --server-log results/${RUN_ID}_server.log --process-match sglang --cuda-so-package sglang --cuda-so-package flashinfer

notes: Use ordinary BF16/fp8 KV first. Do not test fp4_e2m1 KV on Gemma until this row works.

commands.name: start_fp8_comparator
commands.shell: python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model-path ${QWEN_MODEL_ID} --attention-backend flashinfer --kv-cache-dtype fp8_e4m3 --page-size 1 --mem-fraction-static 0.40 --disable-cuda-graph --disable-piecewise-cuda-graph --host 0.0.0.0 --port 30000

commands.name: record_fp8_comparator
commands.shell: python3 scripts/record_openai_serving_row.py --backend sglang --phase before --run-id ${RUN_ID}_fp8 --url http://127.0.0.1:30000 --model ${QWEN_MODEL_ID} --quantization bf16 --kv-cache-dtype fp8_e4m3 --attention-backend flashinfer --cuda-graph-mode disabled-policy-match --server-log results/${RUN_ID}_fp8_server.log --process-match sglang --cuda-so-package sglang --cuda-so-package flashinfer

commands.name: raw_quality_fp8
commands.shell: python3 scripts/openai_chat_smoke.py --url http://127.0.0.1:30000 --model ${QWEN_MODEL_ID} --prompt "What is 2+2? Reply with just the answer." --max-tokens 16 --output results/${RUN_ID}_fp8_raw_2plus2.json

commands.name: start_fp4_clean
commands.shell: python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model-path ${QWEN_MODEL_ID} --attention-backend flashinfer --kv-cache-dtype fp4_e2m1 --page-size 1 --mem-fraction-static 0.40 --host 0.0.0.0 --port 30000

commands.name: record_fp4_clean
commands.shell: python3 scripts/record_openai_serving_row.py --backend sglang --phase after --run-id ${RUN_ID} --url http://127.0.0.1:30000 --model ${QWEN_MODEL_ID} --quantization bf16 --kv-cache-dtype fp4_e2m1 --attention-backend flashinfer --cuda-graph-mode auto-safe-no-graph --server-log results/${RUN_ID}_server.log --process-match sglang --cuda-so-package sglang --cuda-so-package flashinfer

commands.name: raw_quality_fp4
commands.shell: python3 scripts/openai_chat_smoke.py --url http://127.0.0.1:30000 --model ${QWEN_MODEL_ID} --prompt "What is 2+2? Reply with just the answer." --max-tokens 16 --output results/${RUN_ID}_raw_2plus2.json

commands.name: logprob_quality_compare
commands.shell: python3 scripts/openai_quality_probe.py --url http://127.0.0.1:30000 --model ${QWEN_MODEL_ID} --backend sglang --run-id ${RUN_ID}_quality --case short_decode --case medium_decode --request-logprobs --output results/${RUN_ID}_quality_probe.json

notes: Standalone FlashInfer FA2 NVFP4 KV probe results/flashinfer_sglang_linear_nvfp4_kv_probe_genpatch_20260608.json passes tuple-packed uint8 K/V with SGLang-style linear V scale factors on sm_121; treat it as partial kernel evidence only. The autosafe row results/sglang_qwen_fp4kv_autosafe_20260608T1315JST_summary.md proves 1.779x fp8 capacity but fails standardized output quality. The matched d7d931f row results/sglang_qwen_fp4kv_d7d931f_matched_20260608T1548JST_summary.md proves 1.7769x capacity plus decode/extend backend trace and smoke passing, but still fails standardized benchmark quality. Both are intentionally forbidden by the audit until a quality-passing row lands. A claim-ready row must preserve the capacity evidence and pass deterministic quality checks before any speed claim. The logprob quality probe results/sglang_qwen_fp4kv_d7d931f_logprob_quality_20260608T1609JST_summary.md shows FP4 medium_decode diverges at token one while short_decode starts plausibly and drifts later. Next live work should capture a divergence-window trace for the failing medium_decode prompt instead of repeating the capacity row. The native /generate divergence probe results/sglang_qwen_fp4kv_d7d931f_native_divergence_20260608T1626JST_summary.md shows the rendered-template medium_decode path matches fp8 through token 3 and diverges at token 4 by rank reversal, which is less severe than the OpenAI Chat Completions artifact. Next live work should reconcile OpenAI chat prompt/path handling against native /generate before another quality row.

commands.name: record_nodflash_comparator
commands.shell: python3 scripts/record_openai_serving_row.py --backend sglang --phase before --run-id ${RUN_ID}_nodflash --url http://127.0.0.1:30000 --model ${QWEN_TARGET_MODEL_ID} --quantization ${QWEN_QUANTIZATION} --kv-cache-dtype ${KV_CACHE_DTYPE} --attention-backend flashinfer --cuda-graph-mode enabled --server-log results/${RUN_ID}_nodflash_server.log --process-match sglang --cuda-so-package sglang --cuda-so-package flashinfer

commands.name: start_dflash
commands.shell: python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model-path ${QWEN_TARGET_MODEL_ID} --speculative-algorithm DFLASH --speculative-draft-model-path ${QWEN_DFLASH_DRAFTER_ID} --speculative-num-draft-tokens 15 --attention-backend flashinfer --host 0.0.0.0 --port 30000

commands.name: record_dflash
commands.shell: python3 scripts/record_openai_serving_row.py --backend sglang --phase after --run-id ${RUN_ID} --url http://127.0.0.1:30000 --model ${QWEN_TARGET_MODEL_ID} --quantization ${QWEN_QUANTIZATION} --kv-cache-dtype ${KV_CACHE_DTYPE} --attention-backend flashinfer --cuda-graph-mode enabled --server-log results/${RUN_ID}_server.log --process-match sglang --cuda-so-package sglang --cuda-so-package flashinfer

notes: Record accepted-draft metrics from server logs or a runtime endpoint if available. This is a SGLang counterpart, not a direct vLLM patch port.

commands.name: pull_image_with_evidence
commands.shell: scripts/pull_container_with_evidence.sh ghcr.io/aeon-7/vllm-spark-omni-q36:v2 ${RUN_ID}

commands.name: checkpoint_audit
commands.shell: python3 scripts/nvfp4_checkpoint_audit.py --model-dir ${AEON_QWEN36_MODEL_DIR} --output results/${RUN_ID}_nvfp4_checkpoint_audit.json --strict

commands.name: run_aeon_reproduction
commands.shell: CHAT_TEMPLATE_KWARGS_JSON='{"enable_thinking": false}' CHAT_SMOKE_MAX_TOKENS=64 CUDA_SO_PACKAGE=vllm,flashinfer RECORD=1 MODEL_DIR=${AEON_QWEN36_MODEL_DIR} DRAFTER_DIR=${AEON_QWEN36_DRAFTER_DIR} scripts/run_aeon_vllm_reproduction.sh qwen36-dflash ${RUN_ID}

commands.name: run_derived_fork_reproduction
commands.shell: IMAGE=jethac-vllm-aeon-q36:db4b210c1-cleanfa2 RUNTIME_REF='ghcr.io/aeon-7/vllm-spark-omni-q36:v2 + jethac/vllm@db4b210c1 + compressed-tensors==0.17.0 + humming-kernels==0.1.4 + ABI-matched FA2 build' CHAT_TEMPLATE_KWARGS_JSON='{"enable_thinking": false}' CHAT_SMOKE_MAX_TOKENS=64 CUDA_SO_PACKAGE=vllm,flashinfer RECORD=1 MODEL_DIR=${AEON_QWEN36_MODEL_DIR} DRAFTER_DIR=${AEON_QWEN36_DRAFTER_DIR} scripts/run_aeon_vllm_reproduction.sh qwen36-dflash ${RUN_ID}_jethac_clean

commands.name: audit_derived_fork_container
commands.shell: scripts/run_vllm_incontainer_target_audit.sh jethac-vllm-aeon-q36:db4b210c1-cleanfa2 ${RUN_ID}_jethac_clean

notes: AEON v2 and the earlier derived jethac/vLLM row pass with API-level Qwen thinking disabled. The earlier passing fork-derived row depends on compressed-tensors==0.17.0, humming-kernels==0.1.4, and AEON's FA2 binary; do not treat it as clean fork packaging, native sm_121a proof, or a local patch speedup. The next proof uses jethac/vllm@db4b210c1 with VLLM_PRECOMPILED_SKIP_FLASH_ATTN=1, an ABI-matched FA2 build, a no-think Qwen serving row, and in-container binary/JIT target audit.

commands.name: start_fp8_comparator
commands.shell: IMAGE=${VLLM_QWEN_IMAGE} MODEL_DIR=${AEON_QWEN36_MODEL_DIR} DRAFTER_DIR= KV_CACHE_DTYPE=fp8 CHAT_TEMPLATE_KWARGS_JSON='{"enable_thinking": false}' scripts/run_aeon_vllm_reproduction.sh qwen36-dflash ${RUN_ID}_fp8

commands.name: record_fp8_comparator
commands.shell: python3 scripts/record_openai_serving_row.py --backend vllm --phase before --run-id ${RUN_ID}_fp8 --url ${VLLM_SERVER_URL} --model ${QWEN_MODEL_ID} --quantization compressed-tensors --kv-cache-dtype fp8 --attention-backend flash_attn --cuda-graph-mode enabled --server-log results/${RUN_ID}_fp8_server.log --process-match vllm --cuda-so-package vllm --cuda-so-package flashinfer

commands.name: start_nvfp4_kv
commands.shell: IMAGE=${VLLM_QWEN_IMAGE} MODEL_DIR=${AEON_QWEN36_MODEL_DIR} DRAFTER_DIR= KV_CACHE_DTYPE=nvfp4 FLASHINFER_EXTRA_CUDAFLAGS=-DFLASHINFER_PAGED_V_SF_DESWIZZLE=1 CHAT_TEMPLATE_KWARGS_JSON='{"enable_thinking": false}' scripts/run_aeon_vllm_reproduction.sh qwen36-dflash ${RUN_ID}_nvfp4_kv

commands.name: record_nvfp4_kv
commands.shell: python3 scripts/record_openai_serving_row.py --backend vllm --phase after --run-id ${RUN_ID}_nvfp4_kv --url ${VLLM_SERVER_URL} --model ${QWEN_MODEL_ID} --quantization compressed-tensors --kv-cache-dtype nvfp4 --attention-backend flashinfer-fa2-nvfp4-kv --cuda-graph-mode enabled-or-documented --server-log results/${RUN_ID}_nvfp4_kv_server.log --process-match vllm --cuda-so-package vllm --cuda-so-package flashinfer

notes: Separate this from vLLM Qwen+DFlash. Existing vLLM routing probe results/vllm_nvfp4_sm12x_routing_probe_20260607T171227Z.json and FlashInfer standalone probe results/flashinfer_nvfp4_kv_probe_20260608T023901JST.json are partial evidence only. Claim-ready serving must prove FlashInfer FA2 NVFP4 KV selection, KV pool tokens, quality, warmed decode, and a matched fp8 comparator on the same Qwen model/settings.

commands.name: start_server
commands.shell: ${LLAMA_SERVER} --model ${QWEN3_GGUF_MODEL} --alias ${MODEL_ALIAS} --host 0.0.0.0 --port 18082 --ctx-size 8192 --gpu-layers all

commands.name: record_row
commands.shell: python3 scripts/record_openai_serving_row.py --backend llamacpp --phase exploratory --run-id ${RUN_ID} --url http://127.0.0.1:18082 --model ${MODEL_ALIAS} --runtime-ref ${LLAMA_CPP_COMMIT} --quantization gguf --kv-cache-dtype default --attention-backend llama.cpp-cuda --cuda-graph-mode enabled --server-log results/${RUN_ID}_server.log --process-match llama-server --llama-bench-command "${LLAMA_BENCH} --model ${QWEN3_GGUF_MODEL} --gpu-layers all" --run-gguf-logprobs-probe

notes: Use Qwen3 or Qwen3.6-class GGUF, not the existing Qwen2.5 1.5B row.

commands.name: source_checkpoint_audit_if_applicable
commands.shell: python3 scripts/nvfp4_checkpoint_audit.py --model-dir ${SOURCE_NVFP4_MODEL_DIR} --output results/${RUN_ID}_nvfp4_checkpoint_audit.json --strict

commands.name: start_server
commands.shell: ${LLAMA_SERVER} --model ${NVFP4_OR_MXFP4_GGUF_MODEL} --alias ${MODEL_ALIAS} --host 0.0.0.0 --port 18082 --ctx-size 8192 --gpu-layers all

commands.name: record_row
commands.shell: python3 scripts/record_openai_serving_row.py --backend llamacpp --phase exploratory --run-id ${RUN_ID} --url http://127.0.0.1:18082 --model ${MODEL_ALIAS} --runtime-ref ${LLAMA_CPP_COMMIT} --quantization native-fp4-gguf --kv-cache-dtype default --attention-backend llama.cpp-cuda --cuda-graph-mode enabled --server-log results/${RUN_ID}_server.log --process-match llama-server --llama-bench-command "${LLAMA_BENCH} --model ${NVFP4_OR_MXFP4_GGUF_MODEL} --gpu-layers all"

notes: BLACKWELL_NATIVE_FP4=1 in logs is not enough. The model must be NVFP4/MXFP4 GGUF and runtime dispatch evidence must show the native path.

commands.name: native_probe
commands.shell: python3 scripts/llamacpp_native_loglikelihood_probe.py --url ${LLAMA_SERVER_URL} --n-probs ${N_PROBS:-512} --output results/${RUN_ID}_probe.json

commands.name: native_task
commands.shell: python3 scripts/llamacpp_native_loglikelihood_task.py --url ${LLAMA_SERVER_URL} --n-probs ${N_PROBS:-512} --input tasks/llamacpp_loglikelihood_smoke.jsonl --output results/${RUN_ID}_task.json

notes: Live GB10 artifact results/llamacpp_native_loglikelihood_20260608T1331JST_summary.md is negative at n_probs=512: likely continuations are scored, but the unlikely continuation is missing from top-N probabilities. A claim-ready row needs direct supplied-token logprobs, practical full-vocabulary probabilities, or another native scoring path; simply rerunning top-512 is not enough.

commands.name: build_newer_llama_server
commands.shell: git clone https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp ${WORKDIR}/llama.cpp && cd ${WORKDIR}/llama.cpp && git checkout ${NEWER_REF:-master} && cmake -S . -B build -DGGML_CUDA=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DCMAKE_CUDA_ARCHITECTURES=121 && cmake --build build --target llama-server -j$(nproc)

commands.name: probe_max0
commands.shell: python3 scripts/gguf_logprobs_probe.py --url ${LLAMA_SERVER_URL} --model qwen25-logprob-newer --context "The capital of Japan is" --continuation " zebra" --max-tokens 0 --output results/${RUN_ID}_max0.json

commands.name: probe_max1
commands.shell: python3 scripts/gguf_logprobs_probe.py --url ${LLAMA_SERVER_URL} --model qwen25-logprob-newer --context "The capital of Japan is" --continuation " zebra" --max-tokens 1 --output results/${RUN_ID}_max1.json

notes: This is the current GGUF accuracy stop-point. Pass only if prompt tokens and token_logprobs cover supplied continuation token ids [1147,50213]. If both rows still expose only generated-token logprobs.content, move to full-vocabulary practicality or a jethac/llama.cpp supplied-token endpoint fork; do not rerun the known-negative top-512 path.
